meta.title: President to Powerbroker: The Future of Nursultan Nazarbayev’s Kazakhstan
meta.description: Hudson Institute hosted a discussion on the recent resignation of President Nursultan Nazarbayev and the future of Kazakhstan. Nursultan Nazarbayev’s sudden resignation as president of Kazakhstan on March 19—a post he had occupied since the country’s independence in 1990—marked the end of a tenure characterized by corruption, electoral fraud, and human rights abuses. Or did it? - Captured Live on Ustream at http://www.ustream.tv/channel/hudson-institute
